format: Disclosure of every remote API call (QVAC Hackathon submission requirement).

ai_inference.note: All AI inference, embeddings and RAG run locally through the QVAC SDK. No cloud AI APIs are used anywhere in this project.

remote_calls.name: Bitfinex public market data
remote_calls.purpose: Live USD₮ market prices for the trading copilot.

remote_calls.name: Bitfinex authenticated trading
remote_calls.auth: user-provided API key/secret, stored locally and AES-256-GCM encrypted; never sent anywhere except Bitfinex
remote_calls.purpose: Read-only key verification and order submission. Orders are submitted ONLY when NYX_LIVE_TRADING=1 AND the user passes two explicit confirmations. Default is dry-run.

remote_calls.name: Bitfinex TCP latency probe
remote_calls.purpose: Measure round-trip latency to the exchange before trading.

remote_calls.name: QVAC model weights download (one-time setup)
remote_calls.type: non-AI model weights fetch, handled by the QVAC SDK
remote_calls.purpose: Download the on-device model once; afterwards Nyx runs fully offline against the cached weights.

egress_enforcement: src/netguard.js default-denies every non-loopback socket except the explicit allowlist (Bitfinex hosts). Allowed calls are still recorded in evidence/netguard.json with host + timestamp, so the privacy claim is precise: no egress except the user-approved, allowlisted endpoints, fully logged.
